learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 6 of 10 in the Term 1-2 sequence - Lesson 4 of the 8-lesson PowerPoint unit.
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils sourced and manipulated copyright-free images to illustrate their 'About Me' slides.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils add sound and video to their themed 'About Me' presentation.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils know where to locate the options for styles, themes, transitions and animations, understand the effect of applying these tools to a PowerPoint, and can apply styles and themes to their 'About Me' presentation.
assessment_criteria: Each student will apply a consistent theme across all slides.
Each student will apply a slide transition.
Each student will apply at least one animation and explain why they chose it (or chose not to overuse animation).
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Individual application of a consistent theme, transitions, and simple animations to pupils' 'About Me' PowerPoint, with an explicit check that choices support rather than distract from the content.

key_vocabulary.definition: A pre-set combination of colours, fonts and effects applied consistently across a presentation; everyday meaning relates to a subject or topic.
The visual effect used when moving from one slide to the next.
A visual effect applied to an individual element (e.g. text or an image) on a slide.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Support pupils who apply excessive or clashing animations, prompting them to justify each choice against readability.
accessibility: Guidance to avoid high-flicker transitions/animations for pupils sensitive to rapid visual movement

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Ask pupils to find and note, on mini-whiteboards, where they found copyright-free images last lesson.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Recall and note the image source(s) they used.

check_for_understanding.question: Why does an image need to be copyright-free before we use it?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed if most pupils give a correct reason; otherwise briefly re-discuss.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Show where the Design, Transitions and Animations tabs are located in PowerPoint, and explain the difference between a theme (whole-presentation), a transition (between slides), and an animation (within a slide).
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and identify which tab controls which effect.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Model applying a theme, a transition, and an animation to a sample slide, then contrast this with a deliberately over-animated slide, discussing why the second is harder to follow.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Compare the two examples and identify what makes the over-animated one distracting.

check_for_understanding.question: What made the second example harder to follow than the first?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Move to independent practice regardless; use responses to set the expectation of restraint.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set the task of applying a consistent theme, transitions, and considered animations across all slides; circulate to check choices support rather than distract from content.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Apply a theme, transitions, and animations to their 'About Me' PowerPoint, checking each choice against readability.

check_for_understanding.question: Is your theme applied consistently across every slide?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils with an inconsistent theme at the 15-minute mark are shown how to apply it to all slides at once.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Cold-call two pupils to present one slide under the visualiser; class judges whether the theme/transitions/animations support or distract from the content.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Compare their own choices against the shared examples and adjust if needed.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: name one animation you chose and why you chose it.
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pupils giving no reasoning beyond 'it looked good' are flagged for a brief recap on purposeful design choices next lesson.
